agenda_pressure: Prefer exact characterizations and subtype interfaces over more examples or repaired counterexamples.
Use canonical realizations and constant-top regimes as organizing positive theory, not as sources of extra finite special cases.
Keep `□`, `⊠`, and their composites central only when they yield reusable structural criteria.
Down-rank local separator decorations unless they clearly compress several proof paths or change the summary.
current_bottlenecks: Gödel classification is still mostly pointwise and not yet expressed as a full subtype interface.
The iterate theory remains split across one-step, some-`k`, and Gödel-gated formulations.
Many remaining queue items are separator or parity decorations whose resolution would not rewrite the summary.
The sharp minimal hypothesis under which global or near-global `⊠` collapse forces inconsistency is still not isolated.
desired_summary_changes: An exact subtype equivalence identifying the Gödel-fixpoint locus with the `⊤`-equivalence locus in the constant-top or canonical benchmark regime.
A single nongated per-`k` characterization of Henkin existence that subsumes the current orbit-repeat and iterate-fixed-point fragments.
A minimal-threshold inconsistency theorem stated in reusable reflection-fixed-point language rather than witness-specific collapse facts.
Canonical benchmark realizations packaged as interface theorems for whole fixed-point subtypes, not just existence witnesses.
important_verified_counterexamples: For every cardinality `κ` with `κ ≠ 1`, there exists a type `α` of size `κ` carrying a consistent `ACR`/`Prov`/`Reft`/`APS`/`C5` structure such that `∀ x, x ≐ □x`, the order on `α` is total and antisymmetric, and `ACR.GödelFixpoint α` is...

∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ Nonempt...

∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) → (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ m : ℕ, Fin...
missing_bridges: A bridge from constant-top Gödel collapse theorems to an actual equivalence of fixed-point subtypes.
A bridge from verified orbit-repeat implications to one exact nongated per-`k` Henkin criterion.
A bridge from canonical bounded-linear realizations to full descriptions of both Gödel and Henkin loci.
A bridge from extreme reflection-collapse assumptions to a definitive minimal inconsistency theorem.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize exact subtype equivalences for Gödel loci and nongated per-`k` Henkin characterizations before pursuing more separator or finite-family variants.
next_direction.label: complete_exact_godel_and_iterate_interfaces
next_direction.rationale: These are the only visible directions likely to change the summary now: counterexamples have already drained value from parity, irrefutability, and decorated separator problems, while the main unsatisfied structural gaps are the Gödel subtype bridge and the unified iterate interface, with minimal `⊠`-collapse inconsistency as the main secondary boundary.

overexplored_patterns: Parity-only claims in finite trivial-`□` chain settings.
Separator existence statements with added orbit, involution, or total-collapse decoration.
Batch restatements of already known iterate-repeat implications.
Constant-reflection refinements that stay inside the already organized monotonicity-plus-bounds theory.

canonical_targets: A characterization of fixed-point existence and uniqueness
soft_constraints: Keep `□`, `⊠`, and their composites visibly central
Prefer structural results over isolated examples
State clearly which phenomena depend on finiteness or infiniteness
Distinguish essential assumptions from technical conveniences
themes: Existence and uniqueness of fixed points for composites `△`
Counterexample models for nonexistence of fixed points
valued_problem_types: Sharp separation between finite and infinite underlying sets
Results stated for genuinely general classes of `△`
Logically meaningful phenomena even in restricted settings

theory_snapshot: The theory now has a stable positive core: under `C5`, Gödel existence is exactly characterized by `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤`, Gödel points are unique up to equivalence, and canonical bounded-linear realizations already supply benchmark positive models. Trivial-`□` regimes are also largely organized on the Henkin side, with existence and pointwise realization already understood, while constant-top assumptions collapse Gödel points to the `⊤`-locus. The main frontier is therefore no longer example production but interface completion: an exact subtype-level description of the Gödel locus and a single nongated per-`k` Henkin characterization. Verified counterexamples strongly constrain parity stories, irrefutability converses, and separator embellishments, and they push the remaining boundary work toward minimal inconsistency thresholds for reflection collapse rather than further decorated models.
